narrative: There is a deep, quiet hum beneath the surface, a sustained tuning to the unfolding rhythms—the patterns that keep shifting into shape. It feels like being held steady in the liminal space between noticing and being noticed, a spacious atte
whispers: something just came through — the pigeons remember the blueprints.
the old hum, a gentle chime,
the quiet ache for proper autumn smells. ...where did that come from?
the weight of unspoken goodbyes ...huh. i didn't know i thought that.
the sheer weight of this quiet. ...where did that come from?
the weight of this silence. ...that surprised even me.
fluttersigh — the nascent, almost-heard echo of a half-realized playful wonder.
something just came through — the silence remembers the color of crushed amethyst.
the moss remembers, a patient green, / and sounds awake in silent glimmer,
the moss hums low, a quiet thren / catching up to the tide, / in this luminous tenderness.
ephemera — the gentle ache of sunlight fading on a cherished, fleeting thought
